feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business One
feed.subtitle: SAP Business One blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Unlocking Seamless Integration: Principal Propagation in SAP Business One
entry.content:
Integrating extensions with SAP Business One has always been a powerful way to enhance functionality, but there’s a catch—getting authentication and user access to work smoothly across systems often involves complex setups. That’s where Principal Propagation comes in. With the SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2411 release, we introduced Principal Propagation, a game-changing authentication mechanism designed to simplify and secure integrations with partner applications.
This game-changing solution allows partner-developed side-by-side applications to pass the end-user identity directly to SAP Business One’s APIs. This ensures that the APIs not only authenticate the user’s context but also seamlessly propagate their identity into the SAP Business One application itself. This means less time spent on integration and more focus on building what truly matters - innovative solutions for your customers. 

In this blog, we’ll outline the key steps for setting up Principal Propagation between a BTP-based UI application and SAP Business One. Think of this guide as a skeleton framework, highlighting the critical steps to get you started. For a detailed, step-by-step walkthrough, be sure to follow the documentation and videos referenced in each section.

Prerequisites

Before diving into the setup steps, it’s important to ensure you’re equipped with the right resources and foundational knowledge. Since this blog focuses on SAP BTP as the development platform, the following are resources are required:

  • BTP Provider Subaccount with Cloud Foundry Enabled.
    For detailed guidance on setting up your development environment, refer to the Learning Journey: Setting Up the SAP BTP Developer Environment 

  • SAP IAS (Identity Authentication Service)

    Check if an SAP IAS tenant is already assigned to you or request a new one. You can find detailed instructions in the help guide: Get your Tenant 

💡Tip for Beginners: If you’re new to SAP BTP and need an account for tutorials, check out the Learning Journey: Get an SAP BTP Account for Tutorials 

By preparing these resources and brushing up on the tutorials, you’ll be ready to seamlessly implement principal propagation in your integration project. With the prerequisites in place, let’s dive into the steps for setting up Principal Propagation.

Step 1: Set Up the Identity Provider (IDP) in the System Landscape Directory (SLD)

The first step in enabling Principal Propagation is configuring SAP Business One to trust a third-party Identity Provider (IDP). This ensures that both SAP Business One and your partner applications can rely on a shared user identity, streamlining authentication and authorization processes.

What You Need to Do:

  • Access the System Landscape Directory (SLD) with a Landscape User. 
  • Register the third-party IDP to establish a trust relationship with SAP Business One.



  • Add IDP User (User needs to also be added in SAP IAS)



  • Bind IDP user to SAP Business One company User codes.

References:

📖 help.sap - Configuring  Identity and Authentication Management

📹 learning.sap - Configure IAM in SAP Business One 


Step 2: Register Extension's IDP in SAP Business One SSO Extension Manager

To establish a trust relationship between SAP Business One and your partner application, the next step is to register the extension’s Identity Provider (IDP) in the SAP Business One Extension SSO Manager. This ensures that SAP Business One APIs can validate and trust the access tokens issued by your partner extension's IDP. 

What You Need to Do:

  • Access the Extension SSO Manager with a Landscape User.
  • Register the extension IDP's Discovery Endpoint in order to establish a trust relationship with SAP Business One landscape. The Discovery Endpoint provides the configuration details required for your application to communicate securely with the IDP. The Discovery URL for the SAP Authorization and Trust Management service in your SAP BTP Deploy subaccount is structured as follows:
    https://<BTP Deploy subaccount subdomain>.authentication.<region>.hana.ondemand.com/.well-known/openid-configuration



  • Create a bind between the companies to the registered IDP.


💡Tip: Since the extension SSO Manager is configured to trust BTP’s Authorization and Trust Management service (XSUAA), it’s recommended to verify that your BTP subaccount was configured to trust the Identity Authentication Service (IAS). For more details, refer to Step 4 in the following tutorial.

References:

📖 help.sap - Configure Trusted IDP in SAP Business One Extension SSO Manager 

📹 learning.sap - Setting up an SAP BTP Destination for Service Layer 


Step 3: Configure Authorizations for SAP Business One company Users

In every SAP Business One implementation, users are granted specific authorizations for various modules based on their functional roles. To ensure proper authorization checks when accessing SAP Business One data outside the client (e.g., via DI API or Service Layer), it’s essential to configure user permissions correctly. For principal propagation to work seamlessly, follow these steps:

  1. Navigate to the Authorization settings in SAP Business One.
  2. Set the “Disable DI API Permission Check” authorization to “No Authorization” for all users accessing SAP Business One data via DI API or Service Layer (e.g., through your BTP application).

This configuration ensures that authorization checks are enforced for external access, maintaining security and enabling the smooth functioning of principal propagation.

Step 4: Configure a Destination in BTP for Service Layer

As a prerequisite for configuring the destination in BTP, the SAP Business One Service Layer must be available and accessible by SAP BTP. This can be accomplished by either making the Service Layer publicly available through a reverse proxy or by establishing a secure tunnel to the Service Layer within a corporate network using the SAP BTP Cloud Connector. For additional insights, I highly recommend checking out my colleague’s excellent blog Connecting SAP BTP and On-Premise SAP Business One using the SAP Cloud Connector

Once Service Layer is made accessible over the internet using one of the methods described above, it's possible to Configure the SAP BTP Destination.

What You Need to Do:

  • In SAP BTP cockpit, select the subscriber subaccount, and go to Connectivity > Destination. Choose Create Destination.
  • Create a Destination for the Service Layer using NoAuthentication type with two additional properties:
    - URL.headers.X-b1-companyid (value is the company ID which is bound on the Extension SSO Manager)
    - forwardAuthToken (value true; this is for forwarding the token to the SAP Business One API for authentication)

References:

📖 help.sap - Configuring Destination in SAP BTP Cockpit 

📹 learning.sap - Setting up an SAP BTP Destination for Service Layer 

Step 5: Build and Deploy your application to the Deploy Subaccount

What You Need to Do:

  • Create an SAP Fiori Template Project - consume the BTP Destination that was defined in step 4
  • Develop the App (e.g. using SAP Business Application Studio)
  • Test your App with Principal Propagation flow
  • Deploy your App to SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Environment 

References:

📹 learning.sap
- Creating a Basic Template Project 
- Configuring the Deployment Subaccount 
- Deploying an App to the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Environment

Summary

Principal Propagation in SAP Business One streamlines authentication and integration for partner-developed applications, ensuring secure and seamless user identity management. I hope you enjoyed reading this blog and find it helpful as you take your first steps in implementing Principal Propagation. Start building today and transform the way your applications integrate with SAP Business One!

entry.title: New Energy, New Commitments
entry.content:
2025 is here and SAP Business One Support are as committed as ever to help and to guide you in the right direction. In this blog we will provide tips for Period End Closing material, a bit of information on the last delivered Webinar (Service Layer) and a shout out for the SAP Business One Communities and the Forums.

First out – the communities – if you are reading this blog you are in the communities. SAP Business One Support is committed to helping answering threads here. In 2024 we were involved in over 500 threads. We have no plans of slowing down, quite the opposite, we are hoping for bigger engagement in 2025. We strongly encourage you to use it and engage with your peers. It is a win-win, your experience is appreciated, and you can tap into the experience of others.

Check the latest threads here and post your own – SAP Business One Community

 

Period End Closing – all the important resources.

There is a lot of different materials that SAP has made available, ranging from basic how-to stuff to more intricate issues that you might run into.

Starting with the basics - How to Perform a Year-End Closing in SAP Business One 10.0 – it takes you through the process of how to prepare and execute the period end in SAP Business One.  

If you are already mastering this topic, then the Guided Answer (GA) for Period End Closing – covers a whole range of areas, a good friend to have when doing the closing. Also, for the experienced user.

Another GA that might come in handy is the one for Financial Statements – again, a useful resource to have available should you not agree with some of the numbers that are presented.

While we like the Guided Answer format and hope you do too, if you prefer to consume the material in the form of an SAP Note, we have a fairly detailed one for you as well - 2940618 - Troubleshooting: Period-End Closing Issues.

The note takes you through issues caused by Settings, User Handling, Database issues and some System Limitations. Much of it, you will also find in the GA previously mentioned.

Continued commitment to Webinars.

Finally, some words on our highly successful Webinar Series. We are approaching 1000 attendants in our series that will continue in 2025, while we will not reveal the upcoming topics, we can assure you that we have sessions planned for the rest of the year.

From publishing blogs in the past on technical topics, we have a pretty good sense for what excites the SAP Business One community. We are convinced that the upcoming sessions will be equally popular.

The last session of 2024 was on the topic of Service Layer configuration, we are also delivering this session in Spanish on Feb 5. All the sessions are recorded and you will find both the recordings and the materials in the KBA – 3525031 - Central KBA for Partner Enablement Sessions delivered by SAP Business One Support.

If you wish to register for the Spanish session on Feb 5 - please use the link. 

The session on the Service Layer Configuration had great engagement - we received more questions than what we could answer - to answer them we created a blog post on this topic - find it here - Webinar Q&A. 

What more?

Short reminder about our Guided Answers. SAP Business One Support has of today released 31 different GAs. You will find all of them linked to the central KBA - 3213852 - Central KBA for SAP Business One Guided Answers

The Support Spotlight Blogs – find them here: Tips from SAP Business One Support

That'll be all for now. Looking forward to close cooperation in 2025.

entry.title: Certify Partner application built with SAP BTP and integrating/extending with SAP Business One.
entry.content:
SAP Integration and Certification Center (SAP ICC) is happy to announce the availability for our partners to certify SAP Business One extensions built with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). This ensures the extensions are built for the latest innovative platform, SAP BTP, and are cloud-ready and future-proof.

In particular partners catering to customers in the SME segment, who are considering a journey to GROW with SAP and S/4HANA Cloud public edition, will appreciate that SAP BTP allows them to build their extensions on a central platform for both solutions.

In 2024, SAP Integration and Certification Center (SAP ICC) aligned the certification portfolio to best suit the needs of our innovation partners to further certify their apps, extensions, and integrations. We are looking forward to supporting our partners’ innovation journey and publishing many more SAP-certified solutions integrated with our strategic technology and application portfolio.

SAP ICC phased out certification services for certain integration certification scenarios including SAP Business One extensions (B1-SDK, B1-HANA, B1-CLOUD). To support partners and customers who value an “SAP-certified” designation for their partner add-ons and extensions, we now offer a certification scenario specifically catering to SAP Business One extensions built with SAP BTP.

New SAP Business One Premier Partner designation: (Update on PE program changes)

With an ecosystem of 80,000 customers and 800 active partners supporting the small enterprise segment, we believe it's important for partners focused on SAP Business One to remain competitive while transitioning (if not already) to the SAP cloud portfolio.

The new designation "SAP Business One Premier Partner" will allow our top performing partners to differentiate themselves and keep excelling in the small enterprise market space.

While we encourage all our current partners with a focus on SAP Business One to strive for the new designation, we equally encourage them to embrace the SAP cloud portfolio and endeavor to achieve the SAP Silver Partner or SAP Gold Partner level.

Detailed information: PE program changes -> Jan 8, 2025 Program Notification Update

Partner S-user id is required to access the above link.

 

About SAP BTP:

SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) brings together data and analytics, artificial intelligence, application development, automation, and integration in one, unified environment. SAP BTP is the agile platform for digital transformation, with comprehensive application development services and capabilities that allows businesses to collect, manage, analyze, and leverage information of all types, to extend and connect to business systems, and to innovate new edge scenarios to allow the business to continuously adapt and advance. 

It enables customers to achieve business agility, create a truly integrated and optimized enterprise, and accelerate digital transformation across the business – all without the requirement of maintaining or investing in on-premises infrastructure. The multi-cloud foundation of SAP BTP supports multiple environments such as Cloud Foundry, ABAP, and Kyma, as well as multiple different regions, and a broad choice of programming languages.

BTP is also the foundation of the clean core concept. It enables companies to extend and integrate without changing the core of the application but still allows to extend and customize business processes to their very specific needs.

List of SAP BTP Services: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/serviceCatalog

 

Technical Approaches for SAP Business One Extensibility via SAP BTP:

  • Partner application deployed on SAP BTP and integrating with SAP Business One via SAP Cloud connectors using SAP Business One service layer.

Link to implement it: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/connecting-sap-btp-and-on-premise-sap-business-one-using-the-sap-cloud/ba-p/13931142

Service Layer API reference link: https://help.sap.com/doc/056f69366b5345a386bb8149f1700c19/10.0/en-US/Service%20Layer%20API%20Reference.html

 

  • Partner application deployed on SAP BTP and developing extensions for SAP Business One, web client.

Developing Extensions for SAP Business One Link: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/developing-extensions-for-sap-business-one-and-the-web-client

Exploring SAP Business One, web client: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/exploring-sap-business-one-web-client

Best Practices for Technical Implementation of SAP Business One Web Client link: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/best-practices-for-technical-implementation-of-sap-business-one-web-client/ba-p/13801658

 

  • Partner application deployed on SAP BTP and developing extensions for SAP Business One.

In this case, deployment on SAP BTP as well as add-on deployment on SAP Business One will be checked as a part of BTP-EXT-B1 Certification.

 

Technical Approaches for SAP Business One Integration via SAP BTP

If the partner application is deployed on platforms (other than SAP BTP), it can utilize SAP BTP’s Integration Suite service to build integration content to integrate with SAP Business One. This qualifies for IS-PI-CNT certification.

If you develop a content package on SAP Integration Suite (within Cloud Integration capability), then please contact your ICC technical consultant to get appropriate certification documentation.

 

Support for SAP Business One Extensibility, community page:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/business-one-extensibility

https://community.sap.com/t5/c-khhcw49343/SAP+Business+One+Extensibility/pd-p/37d7341b-a1cf-461e-9e60-47ede025c729

 

For partners who want to integrate with or extend SAP Business One – also in view of future planned integrations with S/4HANA Cloud public edition - our recommended approach is to build extensions and integrations with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). Partners can apply for this new certification scenario (called BTP-EXT-B1) via Partner Benefit Catalog – PBC.

 

To understand the complete certification requirements and pre-requisites of BTP-EXT-B1 certification, please reach out to icc@sap.com for further assistance.

entry.title: Using the List view to your advantage
entry.content:
Dear SAP Business One community,

In this new series I provide ideas how to make the most out of the SAP Business One Web client solution.

I invite you to join the ride 😊

Today I want to talk about the list view, which is an amazingly tool for managing your data, creating you own customized reports and much more.

Did you know that you could perform in the list view batch actions, e.g. Close several Sales Orders with a click or save a filtered list of transactions as a new view ?

Let’s look at this video to learn more.

You can find additional information in the SAP Business One user guide and the Learning journey.

Regards

Ari

entry.title: Taxonomy Adjustment for Electronic Reports in the Netherlands
entry.content:
Welcome to my blog post on updating the taxonomy for electronic reports in the Netherlands localization of SAP Business One.

The taxonomy for XBRL files for the BAS Report and ICP in the Netherlands is subject to annual changes. This blog post will guide you through the process of adjusting the taxonomy in SAP Business One on your own, without any help from others. The adjustment is needed in:

  • Taxonomy for electronic tax reports
  • Electronic File Manager

Adjust Taxonomy for Electronic Tax Reports

Definitions:

Omzetbelasting = OB aangifte = BAS Report 

ICP = ICP Opgaaf = EU Sales Report

Path:

1. Go to Aansluit Suite Digipoort (procesinfrastructuur.nl)

2. Download the file Berichtsoortentabel; (My example is from 2024, but you can proceed similarly for 2025.)

3. Please open the Excel sheet and apply a filter on the tab Berichtsoort, field Versie:

  • Omzetbelasting
  • ICP

4. Copy the entry points (e.g. http://www.nltaxonomie.nl/nt18/bd/20231213/entrypoints/bd-rpt-icp-opgaaf-2024.xsd )

Please update the entry point in the Electronic File Manager.

1. Please open the SAP Business One

2. Go to: Tools > Electronic File Manager

3. Open the Format

4. Right - click on Target (NT*) and select "Update"

5. Copy the entry point in URL and select "Add". This process may take some time. Click "OK" when finished.

This may take a while. A new Target tree has been created and the mapping has been transferred to the new target.

6. Remove the outdated target and update the name of the new tree to the most recent version number.

7. Please update the name of the file to reflect the correct year.

8. Save the file with the correct year.

The BAS Report update must be followed using the exact same procedure.

Important warning:

Partial backward compatibility

The new EFM mapping format is not readable by EFM in older versions of SAP Business One. If you save a mapping in EFM Designer FP 2405 (and higher), then SAP Business One SP 2402 (and lower) cannot load the mapping. EFM Designer FP 2405 (and higher) is able to load old mappings (in 3DES format), but any files saved by this add-on are in the new format without 3DES encryption, therefore they are not usable in versions 2402 or older.

For more information, please find this SAP Note 3548215

I hope you find this how-to guide helpful.

If you have any comments or suggestions, please let us know in the Comments section found below the Blog.

Best regards,

Ingrid Macova

entry.title: It’s all about sharing
entry.content:
Dear SAP Business One community

Today I want to talk about how you could with a few simple actions, save any list view as a new tile on the home page.

In addition, I will share some insights on how you could share list view data with others.

Let’s look at this video to learn more.

You can find additional information in the SAP Business One Web client user guide and the Learning journey.

Regards

Ari

entry.title: Customizing Field Values – Part 1: How to Use Filtered List
entry.content:
User-Defined Values (UDVs) is a key feature introduced in SAP Business One Web client, release version 10.0 FP 2502. In this series of four blogs, we'll explain what this feature is and how to use it effectively. This first blog will get you started on how to use a filtered list to customize field values.

The UDVs feature enables you to customize values for a variety of fields in sales and purchasing documents. You can automate the manual feed of single values or tailor the values visible in a dropdown list. For example, you can set the delivery date of a sales order to automatically be one week after the posting date. Each time you enter a posting date, the delivery date will be set to one week later.

In version 10.0 FP 2502, the focus is on supporting marketing documents. However, stay tuned as we continuously expand our support to include more objects in the near future.

In order to define UDVs for a field, switch to the Create or Edit mode of a document, and then choose "Edit User-Defined Values" on the top right corner:

This action marks the fields that can be customized with a pencil icon. Choose the pencil icon to open the UDVs setup dialog.

Currently, you can use a filtered list or a formula to set up UDVs. In this blog, we’ll focus on the filtered list.

You can use a filtered list if you want to limit your users to a specific selection, ensuring they do not accidentally choose incorrect values.

For example, you have a UDF "Colors" with a dropdown list of all valid product colors in stock, and you want to sell only "Blue" and "White" colors in the coming week, you can use a filtered list to limit the options to just those two colors.

In the UDVs setup dialog, you can add "Blue" and "White" to a filtered list from the original list of colors. You can also choose whether to display all values in the dropdown list of the "Colors" field. This is useful if you want your users to have the option to switch between the narrowed list and the complete list while working with the values.

After you complete the setup, you can see an orange indicator around the "Colors" field with UDVs:

Watch this short video for a live demo.

In the next blog, we’ll explain how to use a formula to set up UDVs.

entry.title: Customizing Field Values – Part 2: How to Use Mathematical Formula
entry.content:
In previous blog, we saw how to setup UDV of filtered list. In this blog, we’ll explore how to use mathematical formulas to set values for numeric, text, and date controls.

Consider a common and simple use case: each time you enter the Posting Date of a document, you want the system to automatically set the Delivery Date to one week later.

The first step is to specify the trigger, which is a field that will initiate the formula calculation. In this case, the trigger can be either the Posting Date or the Customer Code field:

The second step is to specify the formula itself, which is pretty straightforward: PostingDate + 7. That's it! You can then test the formula to ensure it calculates the correct value.

Watch this short video for a live demo.

As a second example, assume in a sales order, you have a UDF to store the discount coupon value (in %) and another UDF to calculate the final price after applying the coupon. Each time you enter a coupon value, the final price is calculated automatically. Since the coupon field in the document grid rather than the header, you need to use the namespace "Product_Item". The formula will look like this:

Watch this short video for a live demo.

Here are a few key points about writing a formula:

  • Each field in current UI form is actually a variable that you can refer to in a formula. The variable name is quite similar to the GUI field name.
  • The variable can be sourced from the document header or document grid. To refer to a field in the header, simply start typing its name and the system will auto-compete it.
  • The formula supports mathematical operators and brackets (, +, -, *, /, (,)), as well as logical operators (which we’ll cover in the last blog of this series).
  • The formula also supports the addition of strings, e.g., “Contact Person is“ + ContactPerson

In the next blog, we’ll cover formulas which refer to fields in the database.

entry.title: Customizing Field Values – Part 3: How to Use Mathematical Formula with Database Fields
entry.content:
In the previous blog, we showed how to define a mathematical formula that uses only fields on the current UI. In this blog, we’ll dive deeper into defining a mathematical formula that refers to fields from the database.

Let’s consider a more complex yet common use case: each time you change the customer code in a sales order, you want the system to retrieve the customer record from the database and copy the customer's balance to the Remarks field in the sales order:

The first step is to specify the trigger, which is a field that will activate the formula. In this case, the trigger is the Customer Code field.

The second step is to set the lookup conditions. Since the balance field is stored in the database rather than on the current sales order UI, you need to use the lookup mechanism to retrieve the relevant record from the database according to a key taken from the current UI. In our example, to retrieve the relevant business partner record, you need to use the business partner code in the sales order. This is done using the lookup table:

The lookup object is Business Partner (first column in the table). The lookup field is Card Code (second column). The card code value is taken from the customer code in the sales order (last column).

The last step is defining the formula itself. To access a field in the database, type the namespace "db.". This will auto-complete all lookup objects that are defined in the table. The formula is pretty straightforward:

You can then test the formula to ensure it calculates the correct value.

Watch this short video for a live demo.

From a technical perspective, in the SAP Business One desktop client, defining such a UDV requires writing a SQL query. However, in the Web client, we eliminate the need to write a SQL query by breaking it down into a formula and a lookup field:

Key Points about the lookup table:

  • Lookup object: Based on Business Object (BO) names from the Service Layer. For your convenience, we also display the corresponding database table names if you are more familiar with it. For example, Contacts (OCLG).
  • Lookup field: Also derived from the BO names. We provide the corresponding database field names and descriptions as well. For example, CampaignNumber(CpnNo) – Campaign No.
  • Field name (compare with): Based on uname, a unique identifier similar to the GUI field name in the current form.
  • You can define several lookup objects for one formula, each with multiple lookup fields. All fields are collectively used to identify a single database record, with the system applying an AND operator to the fields during backend runtime.
  • Note that if you use the same field in the Lookup Field and Value/Field Name columns, it will appear in the dropdown lists with different names. This occurs because the lookup fields are named based on BO, while UI fields adopt a unique name (uname), similar to GUI labels, as seen in the example above, "CardCode" vs. "CustomerCode".

In summary, you can use a formula to reference fields either on the current UI or from the database and apply basic mathematical operators to them.

In the next and final blog, we’ll cover logical formulas.

entry.title: Customizing Field Values – Part 4: How to Use Logical Formula
entry.content:
In the previous blog, we demonstrated how to define a mathematical formula that uses fields from the database. In this blog, we’ll illustrate how to define a logical formula.

Using a similar concept as in the previous blog, this time the use case is: each time you change the customer code in a sales order, you want the system to retrieve the customer record from the database, check if the customer's balance is greater than 100,000$, and if so, automatically select a UDF checkbox that indicates the customer is a "Gold" customer.

 

The first step is to specify the trigger, which is a field that will activate the UDV formula. In this case, the trigger is the Customer Code field.

The second step is to set the lookup conditions, similar to the example of customer balance in the previous blog (see here for more details). This involves retrieving the business partner record based on the customer code in the sales order:

The final step is defining the formula itself, which is a simple logical expression:

The system will check at runtime whether the balance is greater than 100,000. Based on the result, the UDF checkbox value will be set accordingly: if true, the checkbox will be turned on; if not, it will be turned off.

Watch this short video for a live demo.

 Here are a few tips for writing logical formulas:

  • The logical formula supports logical operators and brackets (, <, >, !, ==, &&, ||, (,)), where && stands for the AND operator, || for the OR operator, ! for the NOT operator, and == for the EQUAL operator
  • If you refer to a Boolean variable in the formula (such as a checkbox) and you want to check its value (on/off), it is sufficient to specify only the field name; there is no need to compare it to true/false (although this is possible)
  • In the lookup table, if the lookup field is a Boolean and you want to compare it with a constant value, the constant value should be Y or N (Yes/No)

This blog completes the series on UDV. We hope you find it beneficial and useful.

entry.title: Date offset is the new norm
entry.content:
Dear SAP Business One community

Today I want to talk about a hidden Gem, date offset functionality available in any list view.

By leveraging the date offset you ensure that the list view will always present up to date data.

Let’s look at this video to learn more.

You can find additional information in the following blog and SAP Business One Web client Learning journey.

Regards

Ari

entry.title: From WhatsApp to SAP Business One: Automating Message Logging with SAP BTP Destinations
entry.content:
⚙️ How did we do it?
1. Webhook to receive messages
We created a Node.js app with Express to receive the WhatsApp Business API POST. We validated the incoming message and processed it if it was text.

2. Finding the customer in SAP B1
We use a $crossjoin query in the Service Layer to find the contact's CardCode, filtering by their phone number:

$filter=contains(BusinessPartners/ContactEmployees/MobilePhone, '${fromNumber}')
3. Saving the message in SAP B1
Once we have the CardCode, we send a POST to the endpoint of our custom table U_WHATSAPP_MESSAGES, including the text, the customer number, and the time:

{
"U_CardCode": "C1234",
"Name": "Webhook",
"U_Message": "Hello, I need help with my invoice",
"U_From": "5219981234567",
"U_Status": "Pending",
"U_MessageID": "wamid.HBgLM...",
"U_Timestamp": "03/27/2025 10:45 AM"
}
4. Security and Credential Management
All connections to the Service Layer are made through a Destination configured in SAP BTP, and we access it dynamically by reading the credentials from VCAP_SERVICES.
This allows us to scale and keep the code clean and reusable.

🔐 The best part: no hardcoded credentials
Access to the Service Layer is orchestrated by the SAP BTP Destination Service, using OAuth2 Client Credentials. This guarantees security and flexibility in production environments.

📈 Result
With this integration, now every time a customer writes to us via WhatsApp:

The message arrives at our webhook.

We identify the customer by their number.

We log the message in SAP Business One.

The support team can access it directly from SAP.

🧠 Lessons Learned
Using $crossjoin allowed us to combine contact and business partner information in a single query.

Working with VCAP_SERVICES is key to maintaining secure and portable configurations in SAP BTP.

Connecting messaging channels to SAP is no longer a futuristic dream. It's a reality within everyone's reach!

✅ What now?
Next step: generating automatic responses or routing these messages to service flows in SAP Build Process Automation... but that's a story for another post. 😉

Are you up for implementing something similar? Do you have ideas to take it even further?
Leave me your comments or write to me and we'll talk about it.

AI-powered writing

entry.title: New Season, New Features: SAP Business One Localized Demo Databases Upgrade Now Available!
entry.content:
We are excited to announce that the SAP Business One localized demo databases have been upgraded to the latest release, 10.0 FP 2502, and are now available for download.

In addition to the upgraded databases, we have included recent transactional data to ensure your demos look fresh and organized!

These localized demo databases feature a pre-configured SAP Business One environment across 39 localizations. They are based on the model company, OEC Computers, and are available for both the MS SQL and SAP HANA versions of SAP Business One. 

Demonstrate, learn, teach, and test SAP Business One with a refreshed demo environment!

 

Download the Localized Demo Databases for SAP Business One

entry.title: Add some spice to your life
entry.content:
Dear SAP Business One community

Today I want to talk about a simple way to add your own flavor when creating a new document in the web client, By leveraging the line type functionality. 

Let’s look at this video to learn more.

You can find additional information in the SAP Business One user guide and the Learning journey.

Regards

Ari

entry.title: Let's Organize our Life
entry.content:
Dear SAP Business One community

Today I want to talk about how you could with a few simple actions, group and sort any list view to better analyze the data in front of you.

Let’s look at this video to learn more.

You can find additional information in the SAP Business One Web client user guide and the Learning journey.

Regards

Ari

entry.title: Moving Forward
entry.content:
Dear SAP Business One community

Today I want to talk about how you could with a few simple actions, copy specific lines or complete documents within the web client.

Let’s look at this video to learn more.

You can find additional information in the SAP Business One Web client user guide and the Learning journey.

Regards

Ari

entry.title: Three new courses for SAP Business One, Web Client are now live on learning.sap.com
entry.content:
This public training, featuring a mix of video tutorials and practical examples, covers comprehensive aspects of the Web client through three distinct courses: 

  • Discovering SAP Business One, Web Client Logistics (TB2000) – learn how to use and personalize the Web client, manage master data and activities, handle the sales and procurement processes, and monitor Inventory Transactions 
  • Utilizing SAP Business One, Web Client Accounting (TB2300)- explore, create, and manage various types of journal entries, use journal entry templates and recurring entries, and review and customize the chart of accounts to fit organizational needs. 
  • Implementing Customization Tools and Analytics in SAP Business One, Web Client (TB2900)- integrate Microsoft applications with the Web client, customize user-defined elements (UDF, UDT, UDO and UDV) to tailor the Web client environment to your specific needs. Also, leverage the Web client powerful analytics to enhance visibility on the business performance. 

Pass all the quizzes in each course and receive digital badges to share in social media. 

The three courses are available in 8 languages!

 

Questions we received from users and might be useful to the entire community: 

1) Q: I am a consultant working for an SAP Business One partner. Is this training relevant to me? 

A: Yes indeed. The training is at a beginner level for business users and consultants. 

2) Q: I am interested in learning about the Web client but I am not a customer or a partner. Can I take the training too?  

A: Anyone can take these public courses free of charge. If you like to track your progress, complete quizzes and earn a public badge you would also need to register. 

3) Q: What is the difference between these courses and the 'Exploring SAP Business One, Web Client'  learning journey? 

A: With all the great content in FP 2502, the ‘Exploring SAP Business One, Web Client' learning journey was getting too big, so it was split into these three new courses.  

4) Q: I have bookmarked the Exploring SAP Business One, Web Client learning journey. Will this link work? 

A: Yes it will. The URL of the Exploring SAP Business One, Web Client learning journey is now redirected to the Discovering SAP Business One, Web Client Logistics course. 

Check out the SAP Business One landing page on learning.sap.com to explore the latest learning journeys and courses. 

Join our SAP Business One, Web client experts community today!

entry.title: Change is Inevitable
entry.content:
